source.title: Lateral habenula neurons signal errors in the prediction of reward information

confidence_rationale: The most architecturally on-target LHb paper for V_s invalidation. Demonstrates that a subpopulation of LHb neurons signal information-prediction-errors, not just reward-prediction-errors -- responding when reward information itself is unexpectedly cued, delivered, or denied. This is the abstraction step from value-PE toward model-fit-PE that the V_s construct requires. The paper does not bridge all the way to schema-validity but it makes the construct admissible.
failure_signatures: If LHb encodes information-PE distinctly from reward-PE then a substrate failure isolated to the information-PE subpopulation could produce schema-rigidity without affecting reward learning -- a candidate biological signature of OCD-style perseveration distinct from depression's blunted reward-PE.

mapping.source_claim_statement: A subpopulation of lateral habenula neurons in macaque transmits signals resembling information prediction errors, responding when reward information was unexpectedly cued, delivered, or denied; these signals evaluated information sources reliably even when the monkey's choices did not.
mapping.ree_translation: LHb information-PE neurons are the closest existing substrate for the V_s schema-fit invalidation trigger. The construct -- 'the world failed to provide the information I expected this region to provide' -- is exactly the construct V_s tracks at the regional-schema level. MECH-284 accumulator integrates these signals per schema; MECH-269 anchor reset fires when the integral crosses threshold. MECH-272 (state-gated routing) might be refined by this paper: information-PE could be the gate that switches the system from anchor-dominant to probe-dominant when anchored predictions stop being informative.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Bromberg-Martin task uses cues that predict information about reward outcomes, not cues that predict regional dynamics. The translation 'information-PE = schema-fit-PE' is an extrapolation -- defensible computationally but unverified for hippocampal-anchor-specific information. A V3 experiment would be needed to test whether LHb information-PE neurons fire to anchor-mismatch in a non-reward context.
mapping.source_context: Macaque LHb single-unit recordings during a free-choice task between informative and uninformative cues predicting reward.

confidence_components.notes: Nat Neurosci primate single-unit. Mapping fidelity higher than Matsumoto2007 because the information-PE construct is closer to V_s. Transfer risk low because LHb circuitry conserved.
